title: Multi-well quartz crystal microbalance mass and viscoelastic sensor
abstract: In an embodiment, a system includes a plurality of sensor devices contained in a plurality of wells contained in a well-plate assembly. The sensor devices are used in a plurality of oscillators. Each oscillator generates a frequency and a resistance based on a quality of living biological cells contained in a corresponding well. The quality may include, for example, surface coupled mass, density, viscosity, and/or viscoelasticity of the living biological cells. The system also includes logic for measuring a resonant frequency and motional resistance associated with each sensor device and logic for processing the measured resonant frequency and motional resistance. Moreover, the system includes logic for displaying one or more graphs showing one or more characteristics of the living biological cells contained in the wells.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A system comprising: a plurality of sensor devices contained in a plurality of wells, each sensor device having a resonant frequency and a motional resistance that varies based on a quality of biological cells contained in a corresponding well; and for each sensor device, logic to identify the resonant frequency and the motional resistance of the sensor device based on the quality of biological cells contained in the corresponding well. 2. The system of claim 1 , wherein a sensor device includes a quartz crystal. 3. The system of claim 1, further comprising: logic to generate a reference signal; logic to apply the reference signal to the sensor device, the sensor device outputting an output signal based on the reference signal. 4. The system of claim 3, further comprising: logic to identify a difference in phase between the reference signal and the output signal; and logic to identify the resonant frequency of the sensor device based on the identified difference in phase. 5. The system of claim 4, wherein the logic for generating a reference signal further comprises: 

 logic for generating a tuning word based on the identified difference in phase; and logic for implementing a numerically controlled oscillator (NCO) for generating the reference signal based on a value of the tuning word, wherein a frequency of the reference signal is determined by the value of the tuning word. 6. The system of claim 4, wherein the logic to identify a phase difference between the reference signal and the output signal includes: logic for implementing an impedance divider, the impedance divider having: a measured portion for producing a signal that represents a phase of the output signal, and a reference portion, for producing a signal that represents a phase of the reference signal; and logic for comparing the signal produced by the measured portion with the signal produced by the reference portion to identify the difference in phase between the reference signal and the output signal. 7. The system of claim 3, further comprising: logic to identify a difference in amplitude between the reference signal and the output signal; and logic to identify the motional resistance of the sensor device based on the identified difference in amplitude. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein the logic for generating a reference signal further comprises: logic for generating an amplitude word based on the identified difference in amplitude; and logic for implementing an NCO for generating the reference signal based on a value of the amplitude word, wherein an amplitude of the reference signal is determined by the value of the amplitude word. 9. The system of claim 7, wherein the logic to identify a difference in amplitude between the reference signal and the output signal includes: logic for implementing an impedance divider, the impedance divider having: a measured portion for producing a signal that represents an amplitude of the output signal, and a reference portion, for producing a signal that represents an amplitude of the reference signal; and logic for comparing the signal produced by the measured portion with the signal produced by the reference portion to identify the difference in amplitude between the reference signal and the output signal. 10. The system of claim 1, wherein the quality of biological cells includes at least one of surface coupled mass, density, viscosity, or viscoelasticity of the biological cells. 11. The system of claim 1, wherein a first well, in the plurality of wells, is used as a control well for an experiment and a second well, in the plurality of wells, is used as an experimental well for the experiment, and wherein the first well contains a first sensor device and the second well contains a second sensor device, and wherein a resonant frequency and motional resistance of the first sensor device is identified while a resonant frequency and motional resistance of the second sensor device is identified. 12. An apparatus comprising: a well plate containing a plurality of wells; for each well: a sensor device contained in the well, the sensor device generating an electrical signal that represent a resonant frequency and motional resistance of the sensor device based on a quality of biological cells contained in the corresponding well, and a flexible electrically conductive connector electrically connected to the sensor device, the electrically conductive connector transferring the electrical signal from the corresponding well; and a printed circuit board (PCB) for: acquiring signals transferred from the plurality of wells via corresponding flexible electrically conductive connectors, and providing the signals to a device that is external to the apparatus. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, further comprising: 

 a guide for aligning the flexible electrically conductive connectors corresponding to each well with corresponding electrical connections contained on the printed circuit board. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, further comprising: a seal between the well plate and the guide. 15. The apparatus of claim 12, further comprising: a stiffener for supporting the printed circuit board. 16. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the flexible electrically conductive connectors include an elastomer and an electrical conductive component that enables the flexible electrically conductive connectors to conduct electrical signals when compressed. 17. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the sensor device includes a quartz crystal. 18. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the flexible electrically conductive connector is shaped to form a cylinder, and wherein a first end of the cylinder is connected to the sensor device and a second end of the cylinder is connected to the PCB. 19. A method comprising: placing a multi-well assembly in a controlled environment; 

 placing a culture media in a plurality of wells contained in the multi-well assembly for pre-conditioning, wherein each well contains a sensor device that changes in frequency and motional resistance based on a quality of material contained in the respective well; for each well, measuring a change in frequency and a change in motional resistance associated with the sensor device; and for each well, adding biological cells to the respective well after the measured change in frequency and motional resistance associated with the respective well has stabilized. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising: for each well, allowing the biological cells to attach to the sensor device contained in the respective well for a predetermined period of time. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the predetermined period of time is between twenty-four and seventy-two hours, inclusive.
